sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.101 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: We call a group$G$containing an involution$i$a$T_0$-group\/ if 1) the order of the product of any two involutions conjugate to$i$is finite; 2) all 2-sub\-groups of$G$are either cyclic or generalized quaternion; 3) the centralizer$C$of the involution$i$in$G$is infinite, distinct from$G$, and has finite periodic part; 4) the normalizer of any non-trivial$i$-inva\-ri\-ant finite subgroup in~$G$either...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
